title: Vulnerability Report Template
when_to_use: When documenting any discovered vulnerability for reporting

steps.title: Title
steps.tip: Good: 'SQL Injection in Login Form Parameter'. Bad: 'SQL Injection'. Be specific

steps.title: Summary
steps.instruction: Summary: One paragraph describing the finding
steps.tip: What is it, where is it, and why does it matter? Keep it under 100 words

steps.title: Severity
steps.tip: Don't just state the score. Explain why: 'Network attack vector, no authentication required, complete data access'

steps.title: Impact
steps.instruction: Impact: What an attacker could achieve if exploited
steps.tip: Write in business terms: 'could access all customer records' not 'could dump the database'

steps.title: Steps to Reproduce
steps.instruction: Steps to Reproduce: Detailed, numbered, reproducible instructions
steps.tip: Test your steps yourself. If you can't reproduce from your own instructions, rewrite them

steps.title: Proof of Concept
steps.instruction: Proof of Concept: Screenshots, HTTP requests, or script output
steps.tip: Annotate screenshots with arrows and highlights. Redact sensitive data. Show, don't just tell

steps.title: Remediation
steps.instruction: Remediation: Specific recommended fix with references
steps.tip: Reference OWASP, CWE, or vendor docs. 'Implement parameterised queries per OWASP SQLi Prevention Cheat Sheet'
